WW: State of sky on the whole unchanged.
W2: Cloud covering more than 1/2 of the sky throughout the appropriate period.
Cl: Cumulonimbus capillatus (often with an anvil), with or without Cumulonimbus calvus, Cumulus, Stratocumulus, Stratus or pannus.

N: Sky obscured by fog and/or other meteorological phenomena.

Cm: Altocumulus translucidus in bands, or one or more layers of Altocumulus translucidus or opacus, progressively invading the sky; these Altocumulus clouds generally thicken as a whole.
Altocumulus translucidus at a single level.
Ch: Cirrus fibratus, sometimes uncinus, not progressively invading the sky.

Cl: No Stratocumulus, Stratus, Cumulus or Cumulonimbus.

Cm: Altocumulus translucidus or opacus in two or more layers, or Altocumulus opacus in a single layer, not progressively invading the sky, or Altocumulus with Altostratus or Nimbostratus.
Altocumulus translucidus or opacus in two or more layers, or Altocumulus opacus in a single layer, not progressively invading the sky, or Altocumulus with Altostratus or Nimbostratus.

Ch: Cirrus fibratus, sometimes uncinus, not progressively invading the sky.

Cl: Cumulus mediocris or congestus, with or without Cumulus of species fractus or humilis or Stratocumulus, all having their bases at the same level.
